key_insight: Universal MLIPs are best used as fast configuration-space generators, then relabeled with DFT to train material-specific models that achieve ab initio accuracy for difficult reactive processes.
engineering_relevance: This workflow offers a practical route to produce reliable, material-specific atomistic models for simulations involving transport, barriers, and reactivity without relying on zero-shot universal potentials.
method: Compare 5 universal MLIPs across 7 chemically diverse systems
Run long MD with universal MLIPs to sample configurations
Sub-sample trajectories, relabel with DFT, and train/fine-tune material-specific MLIPs; use iterative self-training for the hardest case
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that about 2,000 DFT-recalculated structures are often enough for accurate models, and the MoS2 sulfur-vacancy jump is recovered with only 600 first-principles calculations in total.
watch_item: Performance on standard benchmarks can look strong while still missing target observables, so zero-shot deployment should not be assumed reliable for reactive or high-barrier events.
paper_title: Universal Interatomic Potentials as Configuration-Space Generators for One-Shot and Iterative Fine-Tuning of Ab Initio-Accurate Material-Specific Models
